items.title: Sources: Chelsea hold talks over Jacquet transfer
items.summary: Chelsea have held talks with Rennes over a move for defender Jérémy Jacquet, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Mourinho rejects 'soap opera' links to Madrid job
items.summary: Benfica coach Jose Mourinho has shut down 'soap opera' rumours of a potential return to Real Madrid this summer.

items.title: PEC Zwolle laat Mbayo gaan via huurdeal
items.summary: De winterse transfermarkt is geopend. In dit blog vind je alle speculaties en bevestigde transfers in Nederland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in het buitenland.

items.title: Max Huiberts: 'Er staan een hoop gegadigden te trappelen'
items.summary: De telefoon van Max Huiberts rinkelt maandag onophoudelijk. Trainers en zaakwaarnemers weten de directeur voetbalzaken te vinden, nu hoofdtrainer Maartens Martens de wacht is aangezegd . 'In de voetballerij is het De koning is dood, lang leve de koning ', weet Huiberts uit ervaring. 'Er zijn een hoop gegadigden die staan te trappelen.'

items.title: Ajax heeft Baas terug in Spanje
items.summary: Youri Baas heeft de race tegen de klok gewonnen: de verdediger is inzetbaar voor het Champions League-duel met Villarreal. Ook de net zeventienjarige Abdellah Ouazane zit, voor het eerst, in de wedstrijdselectie van Ajax 1. Dat geldt zoals verwacht nog niet voor Wout Weghorst en Steven Berghuis.

items.title: Vader en zoon op één veld mogelijk: De Guzmán junior ook naar Sparta
items.summary: Sparta Rotterdam heeft de komst van Jaden de Guzmán aangekondigd. De negentienjarige middenvelder komt op huurbasis over van (Jong) PSV en gaat zodoende spelen bij de club waar ook zijn vader Jonathan actief is. Jaden de Guzmán zal in eerste instantie aansluiten bij de beloften.

items.title: Man United stun rivals City; Senegal win AFCON amid controversy; Barcelona lose; more
items.summary: What a weekend! Man United stunned their rivals City in a fiery derby, Senegal won AFCON amid controversial scenes, Thomas Frank is on the brink as Spurs boss and Barcelona somehow lost at Real Sociedad.

items.title: Chaotic AFCON final an embarrassment to soccer
items.summary: Fan clashes, VAR controversies, a missed penalty -- the AFCON final had it all. But not in a good way.

items.title: PSV als vroegste kampioen ooit? Snelheidsrecord ligt eigenlijk in handen van twee clubs
items.summary: De plaatjes van een platte kar met winterbanden (soms met, soms zonder sneeuwkettingen) zijn niet aan te slepen nu PSV maar blijft winnen in de Eredivisie en 'concurrenten' op flinke afstand blijven door puntenverlies. Maar komt er echt een Nederlandse titel in recordtempo? VI zet de details op een rijtje.

items.title: Morant inspired by 'energetic' European fans as NBA expansion talks continue
items.summary: Ja Morant returned to the NBA court in dazzling style in London on Sunday and the Memphis Grizzlies guard believes any player would embrace the delights of playing in a European atmosphere.

items.title: Haas become first team to reveal all-new 2026 F1 car
items.summary: Haas have become the first team to reveal images of their 2026 car as they launched the VF-26 for F1's new rules era.

items.title: Djokovic begins quest for 11th Australian Open title with impressive win
items.summary: Novak Djokovic opened his quest for an 11th Australian Open title and record-breaking 25th Grand Slam with an impressive first-round display in Melbourne on Monday.

items.title: Dalot 'would have been given red card in Europe'
items.summary: Manchester United's Diogo Dalot was not sent off against Manchester City after his foul on Jeremy Doku was "glancing" contact.

items.title: Morocco boss: Lengthy wait time 'disturbed' Díaz
items.summary: Walid Regragui has said the lengthy wait time before Morocco's injury-time penalty "disturbed" Brahim Díaz and led to him having his panenka effort saved.

items.title: Hearn: Fury still in his prime for boxing return
items.summary: Tyson Fury is still in his "prime" and his return to boxing is great for the sport, says Eddie Hearn.

items.title: ‘Who on earth have we just signed?’: Donyell Malen makes instant impact for Roma | Nicky Bandini
items.summary: Gian Piero Gasperini is clearly a fan of the on-loan Aston Villa forward who shone in their 2-0 victory at Torino Was it even a real quote, or only an approximation, a convenient lead-in to columns such as this? After Donyell Malen put the ball in the net for the second time in the first half-hour of his Roma debut, a member of his new team’s coaching staff was reportedly heard asking: “ma chi abbiamo preso?” – who on earth have we just signed? Nobody would clarify who said this, and frankly it did not matter. The phrase was now canon, repeated in commentary and churned across the oceans of online news aggregation. It resonated because Roma’s supporters were asking the same question of a player who arrived from Aston Villa two days before. Continue reading...

items.title: Bayern go into Darth Vader mode as second-half power play floors Leipzig | Andy Brassell
items.summary: Relentless 5-1 comeback win was ominous and made one wonder how many goals champions could score this season Vincent Kompany had warned after their completion of a record-pace Hinrunde of the Bundesliga season that Bayern would have to “start completely from scratch” for the campaign’s second half. The message clearly got across. Poor RB Leipzig could not have known that his players would interpret that quite so literally. On Wednesday Bayern had done the job in Köln; on Saturday in Leipzig, they gave the full manifestation of their brilliance as the evening went on. This became the numbers of the season’s first half made flesh. It is difficult to know what their hosts could have done much differently. Leipzig had been “clearly the better team” in the first 45, as Kompany had admitted. “It felt like they were twice as good as us.” His opposite number, Ole Werner, described his team’s first half as “the almost perfect performance”, and it was difficult to argue. Had Antonio Nusa, part of the excellent collective movement that led to Rômulo’s opener, taken one of the two good chances he missed in that time, then perhaps the discussion would be different. Continue reading...

items.title: ‘Never been more proud of myself’: Van de Donk delights in London City debut
items.summary: Dutch player makes long-awaited debut in FA Cup Midfielder has not played since Euro 2025 group stage Daniëlle van de Donk said she had “never been more proud of myself” having recovering from a prolonged knee injury to make her long-awaited debut for London City Lionesses at the weekend, seven months after signing for the club. Van de Donk had not played since starting her country’s final group match against France at Euro 2025 in July, a month after agreeing to join London City, newly-promoted to the Women’s Super League, from OL Lyonnes. The 34-year-old former Arsenal player came on as a second-half substitute in London City’s 1-0 win at Sunderland in the Women’s FA Cup fourth round on Saturday, allowing her to finally put months of frustration behind her. Continue reading...

items.title: 'We are not top-five contenders' - is January make or break for Villa?
items.summary: After manager Unai Emery says Aston Villa are not top-five contenders in the Premier League, BBC Sport looks at the importance of the transfer window.

items.title: Senegal face sanctions for Afcon final walk-off as organisers criticise ‘unacceptable’ conduct
items.summary: Mané says it would have been ‘crazy’ not to finish game Fifa president Infantino condemns ‘unacceptable scenes’ Senegal are poised to be punished for walking off the field in protest at a penalty award in the Africa Cup of Nations final. The team were led off the field by their head coach, Pape Thiaw, when Morocco, the hosts, were awarded a penalty in the eighth minute of stoppage time after a video assistant referee review, shortly after Senegal had a goal ruled out. Play resumed 16 minutes later when the players returned, with Brahim Díaz missing the penalty and Senegal going on to win in extra time thanks to a spectacular strike from Pape Gueye. Continue reading...
